EN.title: Webhooks
EN.body:
## Overview

VaultWares webhooks deliver real-time notifications to your application when events occur in your environment. Use webhooks to trigger automated workflows, update your SIEM, or integrate with ticketing systems.

## Event types

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Device events">
    | Event type | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `device.connected` | A device connected to a host |
    | `device.disconnected` | A device was safely ejected or disconnected |
    | `device.locked` | A device was locked (remote or automatic) |
    | `device.unlocked` | A device was unlocked |
    | `device.auth_failed` | Authentication attempt failed |
    | `device.auth_lockout` | Device entered lockout state after failed attempts |
    | `device.crypto_erase` | Cryptographic erasure was initiated |
    | `device.firmware_updated` | Firmware update completed |
    | `device.policy_applied` | A new policy was applied to the device |
    | `device.tamper_detected` | Physical tamper event detected |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="User events">
    | Event type | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `user.created` | A new user was created |
    | `user.updated` | User attributes were modified |
    | `user.disabled` | User account was disabled |
    | `user.deleted` | User account was deleted |
    | `user.login_success` | User logged in successfully |
    | `user.login_failed` | User login attempt failed |
    | `user.mfa_enrolled` | User enrolled an MFA device |
    | `user.password_changed` | User password was changed |
    | `user.sessions_revoked` | All user sessions were revoked |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Key events">
    | Event type | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `key.generated` | A new cryptographic key was generated |
    | `key.rotated` | A key was rotated |
    | `key.deprecated` | A key was marked as deprecated |
    | `key.destroyed` | A key was permanently destroyed |
    | `key.expiring_soon` | A key is expiring within the configured threshold |
    | `key.exported` | A key was exported (if allowed by policy) |
    | `key.backup_created` | A key backup was created |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Security events">
    | Event type | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `security.alert_triggered` | A VaultMonitor detection rule fired |
    | `security.anomaly_detected` | ML anomaly detection flagged activity |
    | `security.policy_violation` | A security policy was violated |
    | `security.compliance_change` | Compliance status changed |
    | `security.incident_opened` | An incident was automatically opened |
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Payload format

All webhook payloads follow this structure:

```json
{
  "id": "evt_1234567890abcdef",
  "type": "device.auth_lockout",
  "created_at": "2024-11-15T14:32:10Z",
  "api_version": "v1",
  "organization_id": "org_xyz789",
  "data": {
    "object": {
      "id": "dev_VD-ENT-00247",
      "type": "vaultdrive",
      "serial": "VD-ENT-00247",
      "status": "locked",
      "assigned_to": "user_alice123",
      "failed_attempts": 5,
      "lockout_until": "2024-11-15T15:02:10Z"
    },
    "previous_attributes": {
      "status": "online",
      "failed_attempts": 4
    }
  }
}
```

## Configure webhooks

### Create a webhook endpoint

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://yourapp.example.com/webhooks/vaultwares",
    "events": [
      "device.auth_lockout",
      "device.tamper_detected",
      "device.crypto_erase",
      "security.alert_triggered",
      "key.expiring_soon"
    ],
    "secret": "whsec_your-webhook-secret-here",
    "active": true,
    "description": "Security event handler"
  }'
```

### Update a webhook

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123 \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{
    "events": ["device.auth_lockout", "device.tamper_detected", "user.login_failed"],
    "active": true
  }'
```

### Test a webhook

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/test \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{"event_type": "device.auth_lockout"}'
```

## Signature verification

VaultWares signs every webhook payload with an HMAC-SHA256 signature. Always verify the signature before processing the payload.

The signature is in the `X-VaultWares-Signature` header, in the format:
`t=<timestamp>,v1=<signature>`

<CodeGroup>
```python Python
import hmac
import hashlib
import time

def verify_webhook_signature(payload: bytes, header: str, secret: str, tolerance: int = 300) -> bool:
    """
    Verify a VaultWares webhook signature.
    
    Args:
        payload: Raw request body bytes
        header: X-VaultWares-Signature header value
        secret: Webhook secret from portal
        tolerance: Maximum age of the event in seconds (default 5 minutes)
    """
    parts = dict(part.split("=", 1) for part in header.split(","))
    timestamp = parts.get("t")
    signature = parts.get("v1")

    if not timestamp or not signature:
        return False

    # Check timestamp to prevent replay attacks
    if abs(int(timestamp) - int(time.time())) > tolerance:
        return False

    # Compute expected signature
    signed_payload = f"{timestamp}.{payload.decode('utf-8')}"
    expected = hmac.new(
        secret.encode("utf-8"),
        signed_payload.encode("utf-8"),
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()

    return hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected)

# Flask example
from flask import Flask, request, abort
import os

app = Flask(__name__)
WEBHOOK_SECRET = os.environ["VAULTWARES_WEBHOOK_SECRET"]

@app.route("/webhooks/vaultwares", methods=["POST"])
def handle_webhook():
    sig_header = request.headers.get("X-VaultWares-Signature", "")
    
    if not verify_webhook_signature(request.data, sig_header, WEBHOOK_SECRET):
        abort(400, "Invalid signature")
    
    event = request.json
    
    if event["type"] == "device.auth_lockout":
        handle_lockout(event["data"]["object"])
    elif event["type"] == "device.tamper_detected":
        handle_tamper(event["data"]["object"])
    
    return "", 200

def handle_lockout(device):
    # Trigger your incident workflow
    print(f"Device {device['serial']} locked out — {device['failed_attempts']} failed attempts")

def handle_tamper(device):
    # Escalate immediately
    print(f"CRITICAL: Tamper detected on device {device['serial']}")
```

```javascript Node.js
import crypto from "crypto";
import express from "express";

const app = express();
const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.VAULTWARES_WEBHOOK_SECRET;

// Use raw body middleware for signature verification
app.use("/webhooks/vaultwares", express.raw({ type: "application/json" }));

function verifySignature(payload, header, secret, tolerance = 300) {
  const parts = Object.fromEntries(
    header.split(",").map((p) => p.split("="))
  );
  const { t: timestamp, v1: signature } = parts;

  if (!timestamp || !signature) return false;

  // Replay attack prevention
  if (Math.abs(Date.now() / 1000 - parseInt(timestamp)) > tolerance) {
    return false;
  }

  const signedPayload = `${timestamp}.${payload.toString("utf8")}`;
  const expected = crypto
    .createHmac("sha256", secret)
    .update(signedPayload)
    .digest("hex");

  return crypto.timingSafeEqual(
    Buffer.from(signature),
    Buffer.from(expected)
  );
}

app.post("/webhooks/vaultwares", (req, res) => {
  const sigHeader = req.headers["x-vaultwares-signature"] || "";

  if (!verifySignature(req.body, sigHeader, WEBHOOK_SECRET)) {
    return res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid signature" });
  }

  const event = JSON.parse(req.body);
  console.log(`Received event: ${event.type}`);

  switch (event.type) {
    case "device.auth_lockout":
      handleLockout(event.data.object);
      break;
    case "device.tamper_detected":
      handleTamper(event.data.object);
      break;
    case "key.expiring_soon":
      handleKeyExpiry(event.data.object);
      break;
  }

  res.status(200).send();
});

app.listen(3000);
```
</CodeGroup>

## Retry logic

If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status code or does not respond within 30 seconds, VaultWares retries the delivery with exponential backoff:

| Attempt | Delay |
|---------|-------|
| 1 | Immediate |
| 2 | 5 seconds |
| 3 | 30 seconds |
| 4 | 2 minutes |
| 5 | 10 minutes |
| 6 | 1 hour |
| 7 | 6 hours |

After 7 failed attempts, the event is marked as failed and you can replay it manually from the portal.

```bash
# Replay a failed webhook event
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/replay \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{"event_id": "evt_1234567890abcdef"}'

# List failed events for a webhook endpoint
curl https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/events?status=failed \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef"
```

## Best practices

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Use HTTPS endpoints only">
    VaultWares only delivers webhooks to HTTPS endpoints with valid TLS certificates. HTTP endpoints are not supported.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Respond quickly">
    Return a 200 response as quickly as possible (before doing any processing). Process events asynchronously to avoid timeouts triggering retries. Use a queue (e.g., Redis, SQS) to decouple receipt from processing.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Handle duplicate deliveries">
    Store processed event IDs and check for duplicates before processing. Webhook deliveries are at-least-once; in rare cases the same event may be delivered more than once.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Rotate webhook secrets regularly">
    ```bash
    # Rotate webhook secret
    curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/rotate-secret \
      -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef"
    ```
    Update your application with the new secret before the old one expires (a 24-hour overlap is provided).
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Next steps

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Authentication" icon="key" href="/api-reference/authentication">
    API keys and security best practices
  </Card>
  <Card title="API endpoints" icon="code" href="/api-reference/endpoints">
    Full REST API reference
  </Card>
  <Card title="SDKs" icon="book-code" href="/api-reference/sdk">
    Webhook handling in official SDKs
  </Card>
  <Card title="Security monitoring" icon="chart-line" href="/software/security-monitoring">
    Route webhook events to VaultMonitor
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

QC.title: Webhooks
QC.description: Recevez des notifications en temps réel via des webhooks
QC.body:
## Vue d'ensemble

Les webhooks de VaultWares envoient des notifications en temps réel à votre application lorsqu'il se produisent des événements dans votre environnement. Utilisez les webhooks pour déclencher des flux de travail automatisés, mettre à jour votre SIEM ou vous intégrer à des systèmes de billetterie.

## Types d'événements

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Événements d'appareils">
    | Type d'événement | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `device.connected` | Un appareil est connecté à un hôte |
    | `device.disconnected` | Un appareil a été éjecté ou déconnecté en toute sécurité |
    | `device.locked` | Un appareil a été verrouillé (à distance ou automatiquement) |
    | `device.unlocked` | Un appareil a été déverrouillé |
    | `device.auth_failed` | Tentative d'authentification échouée |
    | `device.auth_lockout` | L'appareil est entré dans un état de verrouillage après des tentatives échouées |
    | `device.crypto_erase` | L'effacement cryptographique a été initié |
    | `device.firmware_updated` | La mise à jour du micrologiciel est terminée |
    | `device.policy_applied` | Une nouvelle politique a été appliquée à l'appareil |
    | `device.tamper_detected` | Détection d'altération physique |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Événements utilisateurs">
    | Type d'événement | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `user.created` | Un nouvel utilisateur a été créé |
    | `user.updated` | Les attributs de l'utilisateur ont été modifiés |
    | `user.disabled` | Le compte utilisateur a été désactivé |
    | `user.deleted` | Le compte utilisateur a été supprimé |
    | `user.login_success` | L'utilisateur s'est connecté avec succès |
    | `user.login_failed` | Tentative de connexion de l'utilisateur échouée |
    | `user.mfa_enrolled` | L'utilisateur a enregistré un appareil MFA |
    | `user.password_changed` | Le mot de passe de l'utilisateur a été changé |
    | `user.sessions_revoked` | Toutes les sessions de l'utilisateur ont été révoquées |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Événements clés">
    | Type d'événement | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `key.generated` | Une nouvelle clé cryptographique a été générée |
    | `key.rotated` | Une clé a été pivotée (rotée) |
    | `key.deprecated` | Une clé a été marquée comme obsolète |
    | `key.destroyed` | Une clé a été détruite de façon permanente |
    | `key.expiring_soon` | Une clé expire dans le seuil configuré |
    | `key.exported` | Une clé a été exportée (si autorisé par la politique) |
    | `key.backup_created` | Une sauvegarde de clé a été créée |
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Événements de sécurité">
    | Type d'événement | Description |
    |---|---|
    | `security.alert_triggered` | Une règle de détection VaultMonitor a été déclenchée |
    | `security.anomaly_detected` | La détection d'anomalie ML a signalé une activité |
    | `security.policy_violation` | Une politique de sécurité a été violée |
    | `security.compliance_change` | Le statut de conformité a changé |
    | `security.incident_opened` | Un incident a été ouvert automatiquement |
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Format de la charge utile (Payload)

Toutes les charges utiles de webhooks suivent cette structure :

```json
{
  "id": "evt_1234567890abcdef",
  "type": "device.auth_lockout",
  "created_at": "2024-11-15T14:32:10Z",
  "api_version": "v1",
  "organization_id": "org_xyz789",
  "data": {
    "object": {
      "id": "dev_VD-ENT-00247",
      "type": "vaultdrive",
      "serial": "VD-ENT-00247",
      "status": "locked",
      "assigned_to": "user_alice123",
      "failed_attempts": 5,
      "lockout_until": "2024-11-15T15:02:10Z"
    },
    "previous_attributes": {
      "status": "online",
      "failed_attempts": 4
    }
  }
}
```

## Configurer les webhooks

### Créer un point de terminaison de webhook

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://yourapp.example.com/webhooks/vaultwares",
    "events": [
      "device.auth_lockout",
      "device.tamper_detected",
      "device.crypto_erase",
      "security.alert_triggered",
      "key.expiring_soon"
    ],
    "secret": "whsec_your-webhook-secret-here",
    "active": true,
    "description": "Security event handler"
  }'
```

### Mettre à jour un webhook

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123 \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{
    "events": ["device.auth_lockout", "device.tamper_detected", "user.login_failed"],
    "active": true
  }'
```

### Tester un webhook

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/test \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{"event_type": "device.auth_lockout"}'
```

## Vérification de la signature

VaultWares signe chaque charge utile de webhook avec une signature HMAC-SHA256. Vérifiez toujours la signature avant de traiter la charge utile.

La signature se trouve dans l'en-tête `X-VaultWares-Signature`, au format :
`t=<timestamp>,v1=<signature>`

<CodeGroup>
```python Python
import hmac
import hashlib
import time

def verify_webhook_signature(payload: bytes, header: str, secret: str, tolerance: int = 300) -> bool:
    """
    Verify a VaultWares webhook signature.

    Args:
        payload: Raw request body bytes
        header: X-VaultWares-Signature header value
        secret: Webhook secret from portal
        tolerance: Maximum age of the event in seconds (default 5 minutes)
    """
    parts = dict(part.split("=", 1) for part in header.split(","))
    timestamp = parts.get("t")
    signature = parts.get("v1")

    if not timestamp or not signature:
        return False

    # Check timestamp to prevent replay attacks
    if abs(int(timestamp) - int(time.time())) > tolerance:
        return False

    # Compute expected signature
    signed_payload = f"{timestamp}.{payload.decode('utf-8')}"
    expected = hmac.new(
        secret.encode("utf-8"),
        signed_payload.encode("utf-8"),
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()

    return hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected)

# Flask example
from flask import Flask, request, abort
import os

app = Flask(__name__)
WEBHOOK_SECRET = os.environ["VAULTWARES_WEBHOOK_SECRET"]

@app.route("/webhooks/vaultwares", methods=["POST"])
def handle_webhook():
    sig_header = request.headers.get("X-VaultWares-Signature", "")

    if not verify_webhook_signature(request.data, sig_header, WEBHOOK_SECRET):
        abort(400, "Invalid signature")

    event = request.json

    if event["type"] == "device.auth_lockout":
        handle_lockout(event["data"]["object"])
    elif event["type"] == "device.tamper_detected":
        handle_tamper(event["data"]["object"])

    return "", 200

def handle_lockout(device):
    # Trigger your incident workflow
    print(f"Device {device['serial']} locked out — {device['failed_attempts']} failed attempts")

def handle_tamper(device):
    # Escalate immediately
    print(f"CRITICAL: Tamper detected on device {device['serial']}")
```

```javascript Node.js
import crypto from "crypto";
import express from "express";

const app = express();
const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.VAULTWARES_WEBHOOK_SECRET;

// Use raw body middleware for signature verification
app.use("/webhooks/vaultwares", express.raw({ type: "application/json" }));

function verifySignature(payload, header, secret, tolerance = 300) {
  const parts = Object.fromEntries(
    header.split(",").map((p) => p.split("="))
  );
  const { t: timestamp, v1: signature } = parts;

  if (!timestamp || !signature) return false;

  // Replay attack prevention
  if (Math.abs(Date.now() / 1000 - parseInt(timestamp)) > tolerance) {
    return false;
  }

  const signedPayload = `${timestamp}.${payload.toString("utf8")}`;
  const expected = crypto
    .createHmac("sha256", secret)
    .update(signedPayload)
    .digest("hex");

  return crypto.timingSafeEqual(
    Buffer.from(signature),
    Buffer.from(expected)
  );
}

app.post("/webhooks/vaultwares", (req, res) => {
  const sigHeader = req.headers["x-vaultwares-signature"] || "";

  if (!verifySignature(req.body, sigHeader, WEBHOOK_SECRET)) {
    return res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid signature" });
  }

  const event = JSON.parse(req.body);
  console.log(`Received event: ${event.type}`);

  switch (event.type) {
    case "device.auth_lockout":
      handleLockout(event.data.object);
      break;
    case "device.tamper_detected":
      handleTamper(event.data.object);
      break;
    case "key.expiring_soon":
      handleKeyExpiry(event.data.object);
      break;
  }

  res.status(200).send();
});

app.listen(3000);
```
</CodeGroup>

## Logique de nouvelle tentative (Retry logic)

Si votre point de terminaison renvoie un code de statut autre que 2xx ou ne répond pas dans les 30 secondes, VaultWares tente de livrer à nouveau avec un délai exponentiel :

| Tentative | Délai |
|---------|-------|
| 1 | Immédiat |
| 2 | 5 secondes |
| 3 | 30 secondes |
| 4 | 2 minutes |
| 5 | 10 minutes |
| 6 | 1 heure |
| 7 | 6 heures |

Après 7 tentatives échouées, l'événement est marqué comme échoué et vous pouvez le rejouer manuellement depuis le portail.

```bash
# Replay a failed webhook event
curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/replay \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef" \
  -d '{"event_id": "evt_1234567890abcdef"}'

# List failed events for a webhook endpoint
curl https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/events?status=failed \
  -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef"
```

## Bonnes pratiques

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Utiliser uniquement des points de terminaison HTTPS">
    VaultWares ne livre les webhooks qu'à des points de terminaison HTTPS avec des certificats TLS valides. Les points de terminaison HTTP ne sont pas pris en charge.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Répondre rapidement">
    Retournez une réponse 200 aussi rapidement que possible (avant d'effectuer tout traitement). Traitez les événements de manière asynchrone pour éviter que les délais d'attente ne déclenchent des nouvelles tentatives. Utilisez une file d'attente (ex. : Redis, SQS) pour découpler la réception du traitement.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Gérer les livraisons en double">
    Stockez les identifiants d'événements traités et vérifiez les doublons avant de traiter. Les livraisons de webhooks sont au moins une fois (at-least-once) ; dans de rares cas, le même événement peut être livré plus d'une fois.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Rotation régulière des secrets de webhook">
    ```bash
    # Rotate webhook secret
    curl -X POST https://api.vaultwares.com/v1/webhooks/wh_abc123/rotate-secret \
      -H "X-API-Key: vw_live_1234567890abcdef"
    ```
    Mettez à jour votre application avec le nouveau secret avant l'expiration de l'ancien (un chevauchement de 24 heures est fourni).
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Prochaines étapes

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Authentification" icon="key" href="/api-reference/authentication">
    Clés API et meilleures pratiques de sécurité
  </Card>
  <Card title="Points de terminaison API" icon="code" href="/api-reference/endpoints">
    Référence complète de l'API REST
  </Card>
  <Card title="SDK" icon="book-code" href="/api-reference/sdk">
    Gestion des webhooks dans les SDK officiels
  </Card>
  <Card title="Surveillance de la sécurité" icon="chart-line" href="/software/security-monitoring">
    Diriger les événements de webhook vers VaultMonitor
  </Card>
</CardGroup>
